summary.intent: Migrate Pipelines-as-Code authentication from GitHub PAT to GitHub App credentials on an OpenShift/Konflux cluster, handling the fact that GitHub App tokens expire after 1 hour while the Tekton git-clone task expects static basic-auth credentials.
summary.outcome: Implemented a solution where doozer generates a fresh GitHub App installation token before each build batch, creates a timestamped secret (art-transient-pipeline-auth-<epoch>), passes it to all PipelineRuns in that batch, and cleans up stale secrets >24 hours old after completion. All 194 tests pass.

repo: art-tools has three builder types (KonfluxImageBuilder, KonfluxFbcBuilder, KonfluxOlmBundler) that all create PipelineRuns manually via konflux_client.py
PipelineRuns are NOT webhook-triggered by PAC, so PAC's secret-auto-create feature doesn't apply
artcd-task.yaml already mounts GitHub App credentials (GITHUB_APP_ID and private key) into the build environment
github_auth.py already has token generation logic with 50-minute caching and multi-org support
Builds can have 200+ concurrent PipelineRuns in a single batch, making shared secret patching problematic
CLAUDE.md workspace rules prohibit pushing to 'origin' remote, only to 'dev'

code.finding: Only clone-repository and prefetch-dependencies tasks use the git-auth workspace; all subsequent tasks (build-images, scans, etc.) work from OCI artifacts, so the 1-hour token expiry is a non-issue
The git_auth_secret parameter defaults to 'pipelines-as-code-secret' and is templated into PipelineRun YAML via {{ git_auth_secret }}
KonfluxClient already has corev1_client (CoreV1Api) for Kubernetes secret operations

workflow: GitHub App private keys cannot be used directly as git credentials - they must be exchanged for installation tokens via JWT signing and GitHub API calls
Tekton's git-clone task only understands kubernetes.io/basic-auth (username/password) or SSH keys, not GitHub App credentials natively
PAC's secret-auto-create feature only works for webhook-triggered PipelineRuns, not manually created ones
When multiple processes might delete the same Kubernetes resource, catching 404 errors (ApiException with status=404) makes cleanup idempotent
Using timestamped secret names (art-transient-pipeline-auth-<epoch>) avoids contention while enabling pruning of orphaned secrets from crashed jobs

friction: Initial confusion about whether PAC's secret-auto-create could work for manually triggered PipelineRuns
Plan mode prevented code execution until user explicitly said 'lets go'
Conflict between user request to 'push to origin' and workspace CLAUDE.md rule prohibiting origin pushes
Test execution triggered Python asyncio event loop deprecation warnings (fixed by switching to asyncio.run())
open_items: Need to create branch, commit, and push to dev remote (not origin), then raise PR to prod - blocked at end waiting for clarification
GitHub App installation IDs need to be configured per org/namespace in production
Monitoring/alerting on token generation failures not yet implemented
Documentation needed for GITHUB_APP_ID env var requirement and PAT fallback behavior
